message:
Fix INSERT...SELECT statement-level atomicity and add WAL abort-cleanup with sealed-lane tests

This commit addresses three interrelated correctness concerns:

1. INSERT...SELECT statement-level atomicity (connection.rs):

   Previously, when an INSERT...SELECT statement was executed inside an
   explicit transaction and one of the inserted rows hit a constraint
   violation (e.g., duplicate primary key), the rows that had already
   been successfully inserted before the failing row would persist in the
   table. This violates SQL's statement-level ABORT semantics, which
   require that a failed statement rolls back ALL of its effects while
   leaving the enclosing transaction open.

   The fix introduces an internal pager savepoint that wraps the entire
   row-insertion loop. A unique savepoint name is generated (using
   trace-id + suffix to avoid collisions with user-defined savepoints).
   If a row insertion fails, the code rolls back to this savepoint (both
   the pager savepoint and the concurrent MVCC snapshot if concurrent
   mode is active), then releases the savepoint. On success, the
   savepoint is simply released. This ensures all-or-nothing behavior
   for the statement without affecting the enclosing transaction state.

   A new test (test_insert_select_explicit_txn_preserves_statement_atomicity_on_conflict)
   validates this behavior by inserting rows that partially conflict with
   existing data and verifying that zero net rows are added when the
   statement fails.

   Minor formatting improvements (line splitting for long method chains
   and import reordering) are also included.

2. WAL flush-lane abort cleanup (per_core_buffer.rs):

   Records in the per-core WAL buffer's flush lane that have end_seq set
   to None represent writes from aborted transactions. These records must
   never participate in durable replay ordering because replaying them
   would re-apply effects of transactions that were rolled back.

   The fix adds a .retain() call that prunes non-committed records
   (end_seq == None) from the flush lane before the epoch-straddle
   validation and replay ordering steps. This closes the abort-cleanup
   gap identified in issue bd-ncivz.6 (test category 7).

   The make_record() test helper was also updated to produce committed
   records by default (end_seq = Some(...)) so that abort scenarios must
   be constructed explicitly, making test intent clearer and preventing
   accidental false passes.

   Two new tests were added:
   - bd_ncivz_1_sealed_lane_rejects_mutating_appends: verifies that
     once a WAL buffer lane is sealed for flush, further appends are
     blocked (AppendOutcome::Blocked) and the existing commit metadata
     remains intact through the flush handoff.
   - bd_ncivz_2_abort_cleanup_drops_non_committed_records: verifies
     that aborted records are pruned during flush and never appear in
     recovery ordering output.

3. Issue tracker updates (issues.jsonl):

   - bd-3plop.5 (SSI serialization correctness E2E test): reassigned
     from JadeKite to RainyBear with updated timestamp.
   - bd-ncivz.6 (per-core WAL buffer unit tests): closed with evidence
     summary documenting the sealed-lane and abort-cleanup test
     implementations and their validation results.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
